prompt: This subclass constructor causes a compile error: 'constructor Employee() is undefined'. What is the bug?
choices: Employee must declare Person as an interface, not extend it
Employee's constructor does not call super(...) — the compiler inserts super() by default, but Person has no no-arg constructor
Employee cannot have fields if Person already has fields
The name field in Person must be private before Employee can extend it
explanation: When a constructor does not explicitly call super(...) or this(...), the compiler inserts an implicit super() (the no-arg constructor of the parent). Person only defines Person(String name), so there is no no-arg constructor for the implicit super() to call. Fix: add super("Unknown"); (or an appropriate name) as the first statement in Employee's constructor, or add a no-arg constructor to Person.
